title: Improvements relating to base transceiver station (bts) operation in cellular telecommunication systems
abstract: Apparatus for use in a multi sector base station is disclosed. The base station includes a plurality of sector antennas and a base station radio for each sector antenna. The apparatus is operable, if the base station's traffic level is equal to or above a threshold level, to cause the base station to operate in a first configuration wherein: all base station radios are powered on; and downlink signals from a base station radio are conveyed to that radio's corresponding sector antenna; and if the base station's traffic level is below the threshold level, to cause the base station to operate in a second configuration wherein: at least one of the base station radios is powered off; and for each sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off, downlink signals to said sector antenna are sent from a base station radio which is powered on.
claims:
Claims Apparatus for use in a multi sector base station, wherein the base station includes a plurality of sector antennas and a base station radio for each sector antenna, and wherein the apparatus is operable: â if the base station's traffic level is equal to or above a threshold level, to cause the base station to operate in a first configuration wherein: o all base station radios are powered on, and o downlink signals from a base station radio are conveyed to that radio's corresponding sector antenna, and â if the base station's traffic level is below the threshold level, to cause the base station to operate in a second configuration wherein: o at least one of the base station radios is powered off, and o for each sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off, downlink signals to said sector antenna are sent from a base station radio which is powered on. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 wherein: â in the first configuration: o uplink signals received by a sector antenna are conveyed to that antenna's corresponding base station radio, and â in the second configuration: o uplink signals received by a sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off are conveyed to a base station radio which is powered on. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 2 wherein, â in the second configuration: o all but one of the base station radios are powered off, o downlink signals to any sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off are sent from the one base station radio which is powered on, and o uplink signals received by a sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off are conveyed to the one base station radio which is powered on. The apparatus as claimed in claim 3, wherein: â in the first configuration: o a different frequency is used for uplink signals received, and downlink signals sent, by each sector antenna, and â in the second configuration: o the same frequency is used for uplink signals received, and downlink signals sent, by all sector antennas. The apparatus as claimed in claim 3 or 4, wherein each sector antenna includes at least two antennas, namely a Main antenna and a Diversity antenna, and â in the first configuration: o each base station radio sends downlink signals to its corresponding Main and Diversity antennas, and o uplink signals received by the Main and Diversity antennas associated with each base station radio are conveyed to that base station radio, and â in the second configuration: o the one base station radio which is powered on sends downlink signals to all of the Main and Diversity antennas, and o uplink signals received by all Main and Diversity antennas are conveyed to the one base station radio which is powered on. The apparatus as claimed in claim 5, wherein separate apparatus are provided for: â converting the base station's mode of operation between the first configuration and the second configuration for the Main antennas, and â converting the base station's mode of operation between the first configuration and the second configuration for the Diversity antennas. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus includes a controller which is operable to interpret measurements of the base station's traffic level and to determine whether to convert the base station's mode of operation from the first configuration to the second configuration, or from the second configuration to the first configuration. 8. The apparatus as claimed in claim 7, wherein the controller is operable: â when converting the base station's mode of operation from the first configuration to the second configuration, to cause the relevant one or more base station radios to power off, and â when converting the base station's mode of operation from the second configuration to the first configuration, to cause the relevant one or more base station radios to power on. 9. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus incorporates one or more switches, and by switching the switch(es) appropriately the apparatus is operable convert the base station's mode of operation between the first configuration and the second configuration. 10. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus is functionally connected in between the sector antennas and their corresponding base station radios. 1 1 . The apparatus as claimed in claim 10, wherein each sector antenna has an antenna feeder cable which connects the antenna to the apparatus, and each radio has a radio feeder cable which connects the radio to the apparatus. 12. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 1 , wherein the apparatus includes a first filter module for each of the respective antenna feeder cables, and the respective antenna feeder cables connect the respective sector antennas to the respective first filter modules. 13. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 1 or 12, wherein the apparatus includes a second filter module for each of the respective radio feeder cables, and the respective radio feeder cables connect the respective base station radios to the respective second filter modules. 14. The apparatus as claimed in claim 13, when dependent on claim 12, wherein each of the first filter modules, and each of the second filter modules, includes a Tx filter and an Rx filter. 15. The apparatus as claimed in claim 9, or as claimed in any one of claims 10-14 when dependent on at least claim 9, wherein the one or more switches are connected functionally in between the first filter modules and the second filter modules, and the first filter modules and second filter modules are operable to 

 block passive intermodulation distortion (PIM) in uplink and/or downlink signals that may be created by the one or more switches or other components of the apparatus that are connected functionally between the first filter modules and the second filter modules. 16. The apparatus as claimed in claim 3, or as claimed in any one of claims 4-15 when dependent on at least claim 3, further including an uplink combiner and a downlink splitter. 17. The apparatus as claimed in claim 16, wherein the uplink combiner and the downlink splitter are connected functionally in between the first filter modules and the second filter modules, and â in the second configuration: o the downlink splitter is operable to split a downlink signal produced by the one radio that is powered on, so that said signal is conveyed to all of the sector antennas, and o the uplink combiner is operable to (collect or) combine uplink signals received by the respective sector antennas such that a resultant/combined signal is conveyed from the uplink combiner to the one radio that is powered on. 18. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus is frequency band specific, and so for multiband base stations, a number of different or differently tuned apparatuses are provided for use in the different frequency bands used by the base station. 19. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein traffic levels at the base station are continuously monitored. 20. The apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein traffic level monitoring is performed by monitoring the downlink traffic on each sector. 21 . A method for controlling the operating configuration of a multi sector base station, wherein the base station includes a plurality of sector antennas and a base station radio for each sector antenna, and wherein the method includes: â measuring traffic levels in the base station; â if the base station's traffic level is equal to or above a threshold level: o causing the base station to operate in a first configuration 

 wherein all base station radios are powered on and downlink signals from a base station radio are conveyed to that radio's corresponding sector antenna, and â if the base station's traffic level is below the threshold level: o causing the base station to operate in a second configuration wherein at least one of the base station radios is powered off, and for each sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off, downlink signals to said sector antenna are sent from a base station radio which is powered on. 22. The method as claimed in claim 21 wherein, â in the second configuration: o all but one of the base station radios are powered off, o downlink signals to any sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off are sent from the one base station radio which is powered on, and o uplink signals received by a sector antenna whose base station radio is powered off are conveyed to the one base station radio which is powered on. 23. The method as claimed in claim 22, further comprising: â in the first configuration: o using a different frequency for uplink signals received, and downlink signals sent, by each sector antenna, and â in the second configuration: o using the same frequency for uplink signals received, and downlink signals sent, by all sector antennas. 24. The method as claimed in any one of claims 21 -23, further comprising continuously monitoring traffic levels at the base station. 25. The method as claimed in any one of claims 21 -24, further comprising performing traffic level monitoring by monitoring the downlink traffic on each sector. 26. Apparatus for use in a multi sector base station, wherein the base station includes a plurality of sector antennas and a base station radio for each sector antenna, and wherein the apparatus is operable to cause the base station to 

 operate in: â a first configuration wherein downlink signals from each base station radio are conveyed to that radio's corresponding sector antenna, and â a second configuration wherein downlink signals to at least one sector antenna are sent from a base station radio other than that antenna's corresponding base station radio. 27. The apparatus as claimed in claim 26 wherein, in the second configuration, downlink signals to all sector antennas are sent from one of the base station radios. 28. The apparatus as claimed in claim 27, wherein the one base station radio that sends downlink signals to all sector antennas is selectable from among all or some of the base station radios. 29. The apparatus as claimed in claim 26, 27 or 28 wherein, in both the first and the second configuration, uplink signals received by each sector antenna are conveyed to that antenna's corresponding base station radio. 30. A base station incorporating an apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 -20 or 26-29. 31 . A base station when operated according to or using the method as claimed in any one of claims 21 -25. 32. Installation of an apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 -20 or 26-29 on a base station. 33. Apparatus for use in a multi sector base station, wherein the base station includes a plurality of sector antennas and a base station radio for each sector antenna, and the base station is designed to operate in a usual configuration wherein all base station radios are powered on and downlink signals from a base station radio are conveyed to that radio's corresponding sector antenna and uplink signals received by a sector antenna are conveyed to that antenna's corresponding base station radio, and the apparatus is operable to cause the base station to instead operate in an alternative configuration wherein one base station radio is powered on and downlink signals from that one base station radio are conveyed to all sector antennas and uplink signals received by a sector 

antenna are conveyed to the said one base station radio.
